body_txt: There is The Corruption , spread by The Corrupter , there are its victims, and then there is the paragon who resists it and stays true to their ideals. While others about them can be tempted by power, fame, sex, money, or maybe love, this character stands firm, though they may feel the temptation and sorely wish for it. They greet fame with Think Nothing of It and often tell people to Keep the Reward . This is a key element of Ideal Hero , All-Loving Hero , The Cape , The Redeemer and the Token Good Cop . They may wield weapons that are Only the Pure of Heart . However, a strict adherence to ideals is not always a good thing, since Pure Is Not Good ; resisting corrupting influences may signify rigid inflexibility in a character, who dismisses arguments about why their held ideals are flawed. The Knight Templar is this type of character played as a villain or Anti-Hero . Female versions may be indicated by debuting in a Sundress of Innocence . Conflict between two such characters would be only on the basis of differing ideals. To qualify, the narrative must show that others around them are falling for corruption or temptation while they hold firm, even though they are subject to it. They also should never do Face–Heel Turn .
